# AI SKILLS / ai-pkgs

AI SKILLS is a registry and package-management workflow for reusable AI agent
skills and plugins. The CLI package is `ai-pkgs`; it installs skills into
agent-specific skill directories and plugins into vendor-specific plugin caches
for Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, and other supported agents.

## What ai-pkgs does

- Restores project-declared skills and plugins from `ai-package.json`.
- Adds skills from GitHub, GitLab, local files, and a future Marketplace
  provider boundary.
- Adds plugins (multi-artifact bundles with skills, commands, agents, rules,
  hooks, MCP configs) from the same source types. Each manifest entry includes
  a `targets` field recording which agents the plugin installs to.
- Manages per-plugin agent targets with `plugins targets add|remove|list`.
- Scaffolds new plugin templates with `plugins init`, generating
  `.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` as the universal plugin format.
- Lists declared manifest skills and plugins as grouped text or `--json`.
- Reports outdated Git-backed skills and plugins without mutating
  `ai-package.json`.
- Updates all or selected Git-backed skill/plugin pins with explicit
  confirmation.
- Pins Git sources as `<ref>@<resolvedSha>` for reproducible installs.
- Installs skills by copying directories by default, or by symlinking with
  `--link`.
- Installs plugins into vendor-specific caches (Claude plugin cache,
  Cursor extensions, Codex plugin cache) with env-var translation.
- Enables project-scope plugins for Claude Code by writing `enabledPlugins`
  to `.claude/settings.json` in the project root.
- Supports project-local and global target directories for `skills add`.
- Supports a fixed global manifest at `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json` with
  `-g, --global`.
- Uses a global Git cache keyed by provider, source, and commit SHA so repeated
  installs can reuse the same checkout.
- Provides `--ai` for strict non-interactive automation with deterministic
  clack-style text output.

## Common commands

Show help:

```bash
ai-pkgs --help
ai-pkgs skills -h
ai-pkgs plugins -h
ai-pkgs help skills add
ai-pkgs help plugins add
ai-pkgs help cache clear
```

Restore skills and plugins declared in the current project's `ai-package.json`:

```bash
ai-pkgs install --agent cursor --force --yes
```

Add all discovered skills from a GitHub repository, save them to
`ai-package.json`, and install them into Cursor:

```bash
ai-pkgs skills add vercel-labs/skills --all --agent cursor --project
```

Add skills to the global manifest and install them globally:

```bash
ai-pkgs skills add vercel-labs/skills --global --skill tdd --agent cursor --force --yes
```

List manifest skills:

```bash
ai-pkgs skills list
ai-pkgs skills list --json
ai-pkgs skills list --global
```

Check Git pins without writing:

```bash
ai-pkgs skills outdated
ai-pkgs skills outdated tdd to-prd
```

Update Git pins safely:

```bash
ai-pkgs skills update --yes
ai-pkgs skills update tdd --yes
ai-pkgs skills update --global --yes
```

Add one skill from an explicit GitHub URL and source subdirectory:

```bash
ai-pkgs skills add https://github.com/mattpocock/skills \
  --path skills \
  --skill tdd \
  --agent cursor \
  --project
```

Install directly without writing `ai-package.json`:

```bash
ai-pkgs skills add vercel-labs/skills \
  --install-only \
  --all \
  --agent cursor \
  --force
```

Install globally without writing any manifest:

```bash
ai-pkgs skills add vercel-labs/skills \
  --install-only \
  --all \
  --agent cursor \
  --global \
  --force
```

Run without prompts for AI/automation:

```bash
ai-pkgs --ai skills add vercel-labs/skills \
  --install-only \
  --all \
  --agent cursor \
  --project \
  --force \
  --yes
```

Scaffold a new plugin:

```bash
ai-pkgs plugins init my-plugin
ai-pkgs plugins init my-plugin --agent claude-code --yes
```

Add plugins from a source:

```bash
ai-pkgs plugins add vercel/vercel-plugin --yes
ai-pkgs plugins add ./my-plugin --plugin my-plugin
```

List manifest plugins:

```bash
ai-pkgs plugins list
ai-pkgs plugins list --json
ai-pkgs plugins list --global
```

Check and update plugin pins:

```bash
ai-pkgs plugins outdated
ai-pkgs plugins update --yes
```

Manage per-plugin target agents:

```bash
ai-pkgs plugins targets add vercel-plugin cursor
ai-pkgs plugins targets remove vercel-plugin codex
ai-pkgs plugins targets list vercel-plugin
```

Remove a plugin from the manifest:

```bash
ai-pkgs plugins remove vercel-plugin
ai-pkgs plugins remove vercel-plugin --uninstall
```

Clear cached Git checkouts:

```bash
ai-pkgs cache clear
ai-pkgs cache clear --provider github
ai-pkgs cache clear --provider github --source vercel-labs/skills
```

## Command reference

### `ai-pkgs install [options]`

Restores every skill and plugin declared in `ai-package.json`. Skills install
into project-local agent targets by default. Plugins install to the agents
listed in each entry's `targets` array — no agent picker is shown because
targets are already persisted. With `--global`, it reads
`~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json` and installs into global agent targets.

Flags:

- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`.
- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory to install into.
- `-a, --agent <agent>`: target agent. Repeat or pass comma-separated values.
- `--copy`: copy skill directories. This is the default.
- `--link`: symlink skill directories.
- `--force`: overwrite existing skill directories.
- `--skip-existing`: skip existing skill directories.
- `--project`: explicit project-local install dimension.
- `-g, --global`: restore the global manifest into global agent directories.
- `--refresh`: refresh Git cache before installing.
- `-y, --yes`: skip confirmation prompts.
- Global `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode.
- Global `-h, --help`: show help.

### `ai-pkgs skills add <source> [options]`

Resolves a source, discovers `SKILL.md` directories, selects skills, writes
selected entries to `ai-package.json`, and installs them. Use `--install-only`
for direct one-off installs with no manifest reads or writes.

Source flags:

- `--registry <registry>`: `github`, `gitlab`, `marketplace`, or `file`.
- `--ref <ref>`: Git branch, tag, or commit to pin.
- `--path <path>`: subdirectory to scan inside the source.
- `-s, --skill <skill>`: select a skill by name. Repeatable.
- `--all`: select all discovered skills. Mutually exclusive with `--skill`.
- `--refresh`: refresh Git cache before installing.

Install flags:

- `-a, --agent <agent>`: target agent. Repeatable.
- `--project`: install into project-local agent skill directories.
- `-g, --global`: write `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json` and install into
  user-level global agent skill directories.
- `--copy`: copy skill directories. This is the default.
- `--link`: symlink skill directories.
- `--force`: overwrite existing skill directories.
- `--skip-existing`: skip existing skill directories.
- `--install-only`: install without writing `ai-package.json`.
- `-y, --yes`: skip confirmation prompts.
- Global `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode.
- Global `-h, --help`: show help.

Project flags:

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory.
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`. Cannot be combined with
  `--install-only` or `--global`.

TTY behavior:

- If no `--agent` is supplied and prompts are allowed, ai-pkgs opens a searchable
  agent picker.
- If no `--project` or `--global` is supplied to `skills add`, TTY mode prompts
  for the install dimension.
- Multi-skill selection supports type-to-filter, arrow keys, space to select,
  `a` to toggle all matching skills, and enter to confirm.

Non-interactive behavior:

- In non-TTY and `--ai` mode, omitted `skills add` scope defaults to project.
- `skills add --global --install-only` installs globally without writing
  `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`.
- In `--ai` mode, all prompts are disabled. Pass `--agent`, `--all` or
  `--skill`, `--force` or `--skip-existing`, and `--yes` when needed.

### `ai-pkgs skills list [options]`

Lists skills declared in `ai-package.json`.

Flags:

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory.
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`.
- `-g, --global`: read `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`.
- `--json`: print machine-readable manifest skill entries.

### `ai-pkgs skills remove <skill...> [options]`

Removes manifest entries by name. Installed skill folders are left untouched.

Flags:

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory.
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`.
- `-g, --global`: edit `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`.

### `ai-pkgs skills update [skill...] [options]`

Refreshes pinned Git SHAs for all or selected Git-backed manifest entries. TTY
mode asks before writing; non-TTY and `--ai` require `--yes`. If any requested
check fails, the command exits 1 and does not write a partial manifest.

Flags:

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory.
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`.
- `-g, --global`: update `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`.
- `-y, --yes`: confirm writes without prompting.

### `ai-pkgs skills outdated [skill...] [options]`

Checks all or selected manifest skills for newer Git commits by resolving each
stored `ref` and comparing it with `commitSha`. Outdated results are report-only
and exit 0; failed remote checks exit 1.

Flags:

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory.
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`.
- `-g, --global`: check `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`.

### `ai-pkgs skills search [query]`

Reserved for Marketplace search. Marketplace distribution is planned as a
backend API that returns skill zip packages.

### `ai-pkgs plugins init [name] [options]`

Scaffolds a new plugin directory with vendor-specific manifests and component
stubs. Generates `.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` as the universal plugin
format alongside any vendor-specific manifests. Interactive prompts ask for
name, directory, target agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex), and components
(skills, commands, agents, rules, hooks, .mcp.json).

Flags:

- `-a, --agent <agent>`: target agent. Repeatable.
- `-y, --yes`: use all defaults without prompts.

### `ai-pkgs plugins add <source> [options]`

Discovers plugins in a source repo, presents a target agent selector, installs
to the selected agents, and writes entries (including the persisted `targets`
array) to the `plugins` key in `ai-package.json`. The target selector detects
vendor directories (`.claude-plugin/`, `.cursor-plugin/`, `.codex-plugin/`,
`.agents/plugins/marketplace.json`) to determine compatible agents. For
project-scope installs targeting Claude Code, also writes `enabledPlugins` to
`.claude/settings.json` in the project root.

Flags:

- `--registry <registry>`: `github`, `gitlab`, `marketplace`, or `file`.
- `--ref <ref>`: Git branch, tag, or commit to pin.
- `--path <path>`: subdirectory to scan inside the source.
- `-p, --plugin <plugin>`: plugin name to select. Repeatable.
- `--refresh`: refresh Git cache before installing.
- `-a, --agent <agent>`: target agent. Skips target selector. Repeatable.
- `--scope <scope>`: install scope: user, project, local.
- `--install-only`: install without writing `ai-package.json`.
- `-g, --global`: write `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`.
- `-y, --yes`: skip confirmation prompts.

### `ai-pkgs plugins list [options]`

Lists plugins declared in `ai-package.json`.

Flags:

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory.
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`.
- `-g, --global`: read `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`.
- `--json`: print machine-readable manifest plugin entries.

### `ai-pkgs plugins remove <plugin...> [options]`

Removes plugin entries from `ai-package.json`. Installed cache directories are
left untouched by default.

Flags:

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory.
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`.
- `-g, --global`: edit `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`.
- `--uninstall`: also clean agent plugin cache directories.
- `-a, --agent <agent>`: scope uninstall to specific agents.

### `ai-pkgs plugins targets <add|remove|list> <plugin> [agent...]`

Manage per-plugin target agents. `targets add` updates the manifest and
prompts to install. `targets remove` updates the manifest and prompts to
uninstall. `targets list` shows current targets.

Flags:

- `-y, --yes`: auto-confirm install/uninstall.
- `--no-install`: skip the install/uninstall prompt.

### `ai-pkgs plugins update [plugin...] [options]`

Refreshes pinned Git SHAs for all or selected Git-backed plugin entries.

Flags:

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory.
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`.
- `-g, --global`: update `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`.
- `-y, --yes`: confirm writes without prompting.

### `ai-pkgs plugins outdated [plugin...] [options]`

Checks all or selected manifest plugins for newer Git commits. Report-only.

Flags:

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory.
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`.
- `-g, --global`: check `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`.

### `ai-pkgs cache clear [options]`

Clears the global Git cache used by `skills add`, `plugins add`, and `install`.

Flags:

- `--provider <provider>`: filter by `github` or `gitlab`.
- `--source <source>`: filter by source, such as `owner/repo` or a clone URL.

## Sources

GitHub is the default registry:

```bash
ai-pkgs skills add owner/repo
```

Explicit source forms:

```bash
ai-pkgs skills add github:owner/repo
ai-pkgs skills add https://github.com/owner/repo --registry github
ai-pkgs skills add https://gitlab.example.com/group/repo.git --registry gitlab
ai-pkgs skills add ./local-skills --registry file
ai-pkgs skills add file:./local-skills
```

## Agent targets

Common agent IDs:

- `cursor`: project `.cursor/skills`, global `~/.cursor/skills`.
- `claude-code`: project `.claude/skills`, global Claude skills directory.
- `codex`: project `.agents/skills`, global Codex skills directory.
- `universal`: project `.agents/skills`, global shared agents directory.

Run `ai-pkgs skills -h` for the full command surface and examples.
